sonnet_review.biasedText: A new study showed another disastrous effect of the pandemic on kids — a spike in gun injuries.
sonnet_review.reason: Matches the judge’s detection that the sentence asserts causation (“effect of the pandemic”) in the author’s voice. It also overlaps with the judge’s note on subjective/emotive wording (“disastrous”).

sonnet_review.biasedText: The article prominently features firearm injury statistics and quotes emphasizing firearms as 'the leading cause of death in children and adolescents' in the opening sections, while contextual information about pandemic policies' broader negative impacts on children appears only in the final paragraph.
sonnet_review.reason: The structural-emphasis claim is defensible: the piece foregrounds firearm data and places broader pandemic-policy impacts at the end. While the topic justifies emphasis on firearms, the framing effect is a reasonable inference.

gpt_review.biasedText: A new study showed another disastrous effect of the pandemic on kids — a spike in gun injuries.
gpt_review.reason: Aligns with the judge’s detection of subjective/emotive language (“disastrous”). The added note about “spike” underscores tone, though the core confirmation is the emotive phrasing.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: A new study showed another disastrous effect of the pandemic on kids — a spike in gun injuries.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: The model did not flag the causal assertion (“effect of the pandemic”) presented as fact; the study reports association rather than proven causation.
